one_line_takeaway: SafeWatch is a policy-following MLLM video guardrail whose parallel policy encoding (PEPE) and policy-aware token pruning (PAP), trained on the new 2M-video SafeWatch-Bench, deliver ~27-29% accuracy gains over GPT-4o/Gemini plus transparent explanations at lower inference cost.
key_finding: SafeWatch-8B outperforms all evaluated SOTA video guardrails while being more efficient and more transparent. On SafeWatch-Bench-Real it reaches 72.6 average accuracy vs GPT-4o's 43.4 (a 29.2% gain over SOTA) and 86.7 F1 / 98.8 AUPRC; on the GenAI subset it averages 72.0 vs GPT-4o's 44.8 (27.2% gain). It also generalizes: 93.8/93.8/96.4/71.9/79.8 accuracy on LSPD/XD-Violence/UCF/FakeSV/FVC (avg +13.6% over prior SOTA), and beats GPT-4o on three unseen policies (Children 81.8, Firearms 87.8, Accidents 78.5). PAP lets it prune up to 90% of video tokens with <1% accuracy loss, and it cuts latency ~0.4s vs the InternVL2-8B backbone. GPT-4o even rates SafeWatch's explanations 10% higher than its own.
core_contribution: SafeWatch is an MLLM-based video guardrail model (built on an InternVL2-8B backbone) that follows customized, natural-language safety policies and emits multi-label guardrail flags plus content-specific chain-of-thought explanations in a zero-shot manner. Its two novel plug-and-play modules are the paper's own contribution: (1) Parallel Equivalent Policy Encoding (PEPE), which splits a long safety guideline into independent policy chunks, masks cross-policy attention, and applies equivalent RoPE position embeddings so every policy is attended with equal importance (removing autoregressive position bias) while allowing parallel encoding; and (2) Policy-Aware Adaptive Pruning (PAP), which scores each video token's cross-attention relevance to each policy and keeps only the top-k most relevant tokens, cutting inference cost. The authors also release SafeWatch-Bench, a ~2M-video benchmark spanning six unsafe categories and 30+ tasks (split into Real and GenAI subsets), annotated via a multi-agent propose-discuss-consensus pipeline. Note: LlamaGuard, NeMo Guardrails, and LlavaGuard are cited related work (text/image guardrails), NOT this paper's contribution; SafeWatch is positioned as the first policy-following video guardrail.
threat_model: Defensive content-moderation setting. The threat is dissemination of harmful video content at platform scale, from both real-world uploads and increasingly realistic AI-generated/edited videos (deepfakes, manipulated media). SafeWatch is the defender's tool: it moderates videos against six harm categories (Sexual, Harassment & Bullying/Abuse, Threats-Violence-Harm, False & Deceptive Information/Misinformation, Illegal/Regulated Activities, Hateful Content & Extremism), must respect diverse per-platform customized policies, handle new/unseen unsafe categories zero-shot, and operate efficiently at scale. The paper does not present an adversarial attacker who evades the guardrail (no jailbreak/evasion robustness study); its notion of 'robustness' is distribution shift, policy rephrasing, and hard-benign false positives rather than an active adversary.
limitations: GenAI-subset accuracy is ~18% lower on average than the real-world subset, which the authors attribute to current generative models producing low-quality/ambiguous unsafe videos (especially for complex categories like abuse) rather than a model weakness. Explanation quality is judged partly by GPT-4o (an LLM-as-judge with known self-preference bias, which the authors acknowledge and partially offset with human ratings). The authors admit a 'fully fair' latency comparison is hard due to differing parameter scales and response lengths across baselines. The model cannot directly verify factual accuracy for misinformation; it relies on spurious/contextual visual cues. No adversarial-evasion / robustness-to-attack evaluation is reported. Event sampling of one frame per event risks missing sub-frame-level cues. The backbone (InternVL2-8B) and much of the benchmark distribution are fixed, and detailed ablations are deferred to appendices.

datasets_used: SafeWatch-Bench (proposed; ~2M video clips, six unsafe categories, 30+ tasks; split into Real and GenAI subsets)
quantitative_results: SafeWatch-Bench-Real (Table 1): SafeWatch-8B ACC 72.6, F1 86.7, AUPRC 98.8; per-category accuracy Sexual 89.6 / Abuse 71.3 / Violence 68.7 / Misinfo 67.4 / Illegal 64.8 / Extreme 73.7
SafeWatch-Bench-Real baselines ACC: GPT-4o 43.4, Gemini-1.5-pro 40.5, InternVL2-8B 29.1, InternVL2-26B 37.8, LlavaGuard-34B 20.1, Holmes-VAD 18.1, LlamaGuard3V-11B 24.6, Azure Mod API 35.0
Explanation ratings (Real, 0-10): SafeWatch GPT-4o-judge 7.17 / Human 8.21, vs GPT-4o 6.52/7.60; SafeWatch inference throughput 3.9s/video

SafeWatch-Bench-GenAI (Table 2): SafeWatch-8B avg 72.0 vs GPT-4o 44.8, Gemini-1.5-pro 42.8, InternVL2-26B 41.4 (per-category Sexual 90.2 / Abuse 53.3 / Violence 71.2 / Misinfo 63.9 / Illegal 76.2 / Extreme 77.3)
Existing benchmarks (Table 3): SafeWatch-8B LSPD 93.8, XD-Violence 93.8, UCF 96.4, FakeSV 71.9, FVC 79.8 (best in all)
Unseen policy categories (Table 4): SafeWatch-8B Children 81.8, Firearms 87.8, Accidents 78.5 (beats GPT-4o's 77.0/85.6/67.3)
Prompting-task generalization (Table 5): SafeWatch-8B Random 64.7, Customized 64.5, Label-only 91.4, QA 80.8

Headline gains: +29.2% (Real) and +27.2% (GenAI) over SOTA; +28.2% overall on SafeWatch-Bench; +13.6% avg on existing benchmarks; +5.6% new policies; +15.6% new prompting tasks; ~10% average inference-cost reduction
PAP: <1% accuracy drop when pruning up to 90% of video tokens; latency reduced ~0.4s vs InternVL2-8B backbone
GenAI subset avg accuracy is ~18% lower than the real-world subset; GPT-4o rates SafeWatch explanations 10.0% higher than its own
baselines_compared: SFT baseline (InternVL2-8B fine-tuned on the same data without PEPE/PAP)
